preamble.summary: The Temple Sermon. LXX is shorter, especially in the list of violations.
preamble.notable_variants: LXX lacks some of the expansive phrases in the MT's Temple Sermon. V. 4 — LXX lacks the triple 'Temple of the LORD' which MT has as הֵיכַל יְהוָה הֵיכַל יְהוָה הֵיכַל יְהוָה.

variant_notes: LXX and MT are substantially identical here.
LXX and MT are substantially identical here.
LXX and MT are substantially identical here.

verses.mt_reading: אַל־תִּבְטְחוּ לָכֶם אֶל־דִּבְרֵי הַשֶּׁקֶר לֵאמֹר הֵיכַל יְהוָה הֵיכַל יְהוָה הֵיכַל יְהוָה הֵמָּה
verses.lxx_reading: μὴ πεποίθατε ἐφ᾽ ἑαυτοῖς ἐπὶ λόγοις ψευδέσιν ὅτι οὐ μὴ ὠφελήσωσιν ὑμᾶς
verses.variant_rendering: Do not trust in lying words, saying: 'The temple of the LORD, the temple of the LORD, the temple of the LORD are these.'
verses.mt_rendering: Do not trust yourselves in lying words, for they shall not profit you at all.
variant_notes: MT has the famous triple repetition 'The temple of the LORD, the temple of the LORD, the temple of the LORD are these.' LXX reads only the double: 'It is the temple of the Lord, the temple of the Lord.' The triple form in MT is more emphatic and may be a later expansion for rhetorical force. The shorter LXX reading may preserve the earlier form.
LXX and MT are substantially identical here.
